title: React DevTools
content:
### React DevTools

React DevTools is a browser extension for inspecting React component trees, profiling performance, and debugging state and props.

---

### Installation

Install the React DevTools browser extension:

- Chrome Web Store: React Developer Tools
- Firefox Add-ons: React Developer Tools
- Edge Add-ons: React Developer Tools

### Components Tab

The Components tab displays the component tree with:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Component hierarchy | Tree view of rendered components |
| Props | Current props for selected component |
| State | Current state values (useState, useReducer) |
| Hooks | All hooks and their current values |
| Source | File location of the component |
| Rendered by | Parent that triggered the render |

### Profiler Tab

The Profiler records render performance:

```jsx
import { Profiler } from 'react';

function onRender(id, phase, actualDuration) {
  console.log(`${id} ${phase}: ${actualDuration}ms`);
}

<Profiler id="Navigation" onRender={onRender}>
  <Navigation />
</Profiler>
```

### Using the Profiler

1. Click the record button
2. Interact with your app
3. Stop recording
4. Analyze the flamegraph

### DevTools Tips

| Action | Benefit |
|--------|---------|
| Inspect props | Verify data flow |
| Check hooks state | Debug state issues |
| Profile renders | Find performance bottlenecks |
| Search components | Quickly find components in the tree |

> **Key Point:** React DevTools is essential for debugging React apps. Use the Components tab to inspect state and props. Use the Profiler to identify unnecessary re-renders and optimize performance.

quiz.question: Which DevTools tab profiles component render performance?
What can you inspect in the Components tab?
options: Props, state, hooks, and component tree
